title: fix(release): remove dead skipNpm config
summary: The core fix is clean and correct: `skipNpm` is removed from both `Config` and `ResolvedConfig` schema classes and the `load()` function, the README is updated, and the regression test is well-constructed (checking both schema `fields` and the README text). However, the PR bundles two unrelated additions â€” a batch of static utility methods and an unexplained `ordered = false as const` marker â€” that are not present on any other `Schema.Class` subclass in the codebase and are not mentioned in the PR description.

findings.title: `static ordered = false as const` is unexplained and has no consumer
findings.body: Both `Config` and `ResolvedConfig` gain `static ordered = false as const`. A codebase-wide search finds zero reads of `.ordered` on any schema class â€” the only occurrence of `ordered` as a name is a local variable in `execute.ts`. `ordered` is not a standard Effect `Schema.Class` property, nor is it an established convention in this repo (no other Schema.Class subclass has it). Adding an unexplained boolean marker to production schema classes is a smell: it either implies a planned catalog/ordering feature that was never wired up, or it's dead code that leaked from a draft. Should be removed or explained with a comment and a consumer.

findings.title: Out-of-scope static delegators applied inconsistently
findings.body: Both `Config` and `ResolvedConfig` gain six new static delegators (`is`, `decode`, `decodeSync`, `encode`, `encodeSync`, `equivalence`) that are unrelated to the `skipNpm` removal. While `static is` is an established pattern throughout the repo, the `decode/encode/equivalence` delegators are not â€” no other `Schema.Class` subclass in the release package (`Publishing`, `Operator`, `ResolvedOperator`, `LintConfig.Config`, etc.) has them. The PR silently introduces a new convention on only two classes, creating inconsistency. These additions should either be a separate PR that applies the pattern uniformly across all release schema classes, or they should be omitted here.

residual_risks: If future code reads `Config.ordered` or `ResolvedConfig.ordered` expecting it to reflect actual schema ordering semantics, the hardcoded `false` will silently misbehave.

The partial application of `decode/encode/equivalence` delegators may cause confusion when callers need the same ergonomics on `Publishing`, `Operator`, or lint config classes â€” they won't exist there.
